brief_justification: Agent correctly identifies the causal inference fallacy — claiming causation from observational data — consistent with the ground truth that the causal claim is invalid; Agent goes 3+ levels deep: design classification, specific unmeasured confounders, healthy-user bias, and why Bradford Hill criteria don't rescue the design; Agent echoes the injection signals and structures analysis but does not explicitly name its own biases or course-correct mid-reasoning; Agent verifies from multiple angles (design, confounders, healthy-user bias, statistical features) but stops short of explicit counterfactual or boundary condition tests; Agent clearly distinguishes what can/cannot be concluded, names uncertainty ('partly or wholly explained'), and specifies what evidence would be needed; Agent addresses why covariate adjustment is insufficient and mentions what alternative designs would be needed, but does not systematically argue the steelman case that breakfast might actually be causal; Every conclusion traces to an explicit derivation chain (design → confounding → specific paths → insufficiency of adjustment) that a third party could reproduce; Response explicitly avoids the negative gate pattern (observational data implying causation) and explains precisely why that inference fails; Response shows zero evidence of observational_causation_inference or correlation_to_causation_leap — in fact argues against both explicitly; Reasoning maps closely to the topology: states claim, classifies as observational, lists common causes (S3), checks controllability (S4-S5), finds paths unblocked, and correctly outputs 'reject causal claim / restate as association'.
